target.title: feat(events): add audience field for hidden runtime-context system events
target.body:
## Summary

Addresses part of #69492 (system-event-shape consumer-path leakage; under umbrella #69208 Track B).

Adds an optional `audience: 'internal' | 'user-facing'` field to `SystemEvent`, defaulting to `'user-facing'` (full backward-compat for every existing caller). Internal-audience events are wrapped at the consumer integration point in the existing `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` delimiters with the canonical header lines from `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`. The agent runtime sees the content as runtime context; user-facing surfaces strip it via the already-installed `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers (`sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`, `memory-host-sdk/host/session-files.ts`, `agents/internal-events.ts`, history readers, Control UI extract).

Mirrors the producer-side runtime-context delimiter pattern landed in:
- `e918e5f75c` (#71761) — agent runtime-context custom entry
- `6e985a421d` — webchat runtime-context

No new persistence shape, no consumer-side filter (avoids the audit-bypass concern flagged on #69217), no display-layer per-surface patch (avoids the rejection pattern from #69366).

## Why this scope is tight

This primitive is the **hidden runtime-context lane only**. It is not a delivery-routing primitive. Events with a positive user delivery contract (exec completion via `notifyOnExit`, cron payloads, heartbeat acks) are NOT migrated — those have heartbeat-driven explicit-relay paths (`buildExecEventPrompt` / `buildCronEventPrompt`) plus tactical producer-side skips like `bd60df3e53` (`fix: silence cron exec completion noise`). Marking them internal would suppress delivery on regular reply turns where the model is told to keep wrapped content private.

The user-deliverable noise question stays open; the maintainer's recent pattern is case-by-case (`bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`), and that lane should remain.

## Changes

- `src/infra/system-events.ts` — add `SystemEventAudience` type and optional `audience` field on `SystemEvent` + `SystemEventOptions`. Default `"user-facing"`. JSDoc explicitly documents the scope (hidden runtime-context, not delivery routing) and the operator-surface caveat (`peekSystemEvents`/`peekSystemEventEntries` keep showing internal events for debugging). `areSystemEventsEqual` honors the field so duplicate-suppression and `consumeSystemEventEntries` prefix-match keep working.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session-system-events.ts` — partition the drain output into user-facing lines and internal lines. User-facing lines emit as plain `System: ...` (unchanged). Internal lines are wrapped in `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` with the canonical header (matching `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`). `escapeInternalRuntimeContextDelimiters` handles adversarial inner tokens.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts` — migrate `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` to `audience: "internal"`. This event is queued only after the isolated agent run has already delivered to the user via its own channel (gated on `delivered === true` and `shouldQueueCronAwareness`); the main session needs hidden context, not a fake-user `System (untrusted): ...` bubble.

## Explicitly NOT migrated

- `maybeNotifyOnExit` (exec completion) — `notifyOnExit` delivery contract.
- `executeMainSessionCronJob` (cron payload) — user-scheduled reminder content.
- Cron auto-disable warning, cron failure alert — user-relevant.
- Cron `wake()` API — exposes arbitrary user-supplied text via gateway `cron.wake`.
- Gateway presence (`server-methods/system.ts`), node events (`server-node-events.ts`).
- Task registry, session-maintenance-warning, plugin runtime — caller-dependent / operator-facing / third-party.

JSDoc on `SystemEventAudience` explicitly excludes these from the lane and points at the existing delivery paths.

## Follow-up plan (out of scope for this PR)

This PR is intentionally one-step. It introduces the primitive and migrates exactly one producer (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) so the wrap-on-drain integration point can be reviewed against a real call site. No additional audience-PR is pre-committed; future migrations should be evaluated case-by-case as concrete leakage reports surface.

**Candidates that may earn `audience: "internal"` later if a real need is demonstrated:** debug- or awareness-style producers that are queued *after* the user has already received delivery on a different channel (the same shape as `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` here). Each would need its own justification — no batch migration.

**Explicitly NOT in any audience-PR sequence** (these stay in the maintainer's case-by-case tactical-patch lane, e.g. `bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`):
- exec completion (`maybeNotifyOnExit`)
- cron payload delivery (`executeMainSessionCronJob`)
- heartbeat acks
- any other producer with a positive user delivery contract

A larger abstraction was floated during peer review — a 3-state shape (`prompt-visible` | `hidden-context` | `dedicated-delivery`) for events that have both an internal-awareness *and* a user-delivery contract. **Not on the roadmap.** That redesign collides with the heartbeat-runner explicit-relay paths and the producer-side skips the maintainer has been adding directly; pursuing it would re-open the rejection patterns from #69366. Flagging here only so future readers know it was considered and deliberately not pursued.

If maintainer direction differs, happy to follow guidance on #69492 before landing further migrations.


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: cron-awareness system events (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`, queued onto the main session **after** the isolated cron has already delivered the result on its own channel) were appearing in the main session as `System (untrusted): cron run …` user-visible bubbles, even though their purpose is internal awareness for the agent rather than a user-deliverable message. The fix migrates that producer to `audience: "internal"` so the autoreply integration site wraps the event in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` … `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` and the existing `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers remove it from every user-facing surface.
- Real environment tested: production OpenClaw gateway daemon on macOS Sonoma (Darwin 23.6.0), Node 25.6.0, running multiple isolated cron jobs against an 11-account Matrix deployment. Patched dist (built from the 2-commit local branch on top of `mercury/local-patches`) has been running continuously since reload.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built patched dist with `pnpm openclaw build`'s equivalent (`pnpm build`), reloaded the gateway LaunchDaemon (`sudo launchctl bootout system/ai.openclaw.gateway && sudo launchctl bootstrap system /Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`), and let scheduled isolated cron jobs (`~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`) execute on their normal schedules (`mercury` agent, gpt-5-mini / claude-sonnet-4-6 backends, Matrix delivery target).
- Evidence after fix:
  - **The wrap delimiters are already a proven primitive in the running gateway** — this PR adds a new producer to that lane. Live transcript excerpt from `~/.openclaw/agents/devin/sessions/3998fa7e-…03d.jsonl` showing the `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` / `OpenClaw runtime context (internal):` block landing in the user-message context (auto-stripped from user-visible surfaces by `stripInternalRuntimeContext`) — terminal output below:
    ```
    [Sun 2026-05-03 21:14 EDT] <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    [Internal task completion event]
    source: subagent
    session_key: agent:devin:subagent:9d4f2234-…
    ```
  - **Cron isolated runs completing successfully on the patched daemon** — redacted runtime log excerpt from `~/.openclaw/cron/runs/4309f6e7-f09d-4011-93ff-21726e3aa76d.jsonl` (the actual openclaw cron run record):
    ```json
    {"action":"finished","status":"ok","summary":"Top hit: openclaw-docs/automation/cron-jobs … [mercury | claude-sonnet-4-6]","sessionKey":"agent:mercury:cron:4309f6e7-…","durationMs":27268,"model":"gpt-5-mini","provider":"openai"}
    ```
    These are the exact runs whose post-delivery `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` calls produce the events this PR migrates to `audience: "internal"`. Neither the cron run itself nor the main session has thrown since the rebased dist was deployed.
  - Gateway uptime: the `openclaw gateway` daemon has been continuously up since the patched-dist reload, processing real ws traffic — `agents.list`, `chat.history`, `sessions.list` round-trips all 400-500ms in `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log`; `[sessions/store] pruned stale session entries` (an adjacent local patch) firing on schedule.
  - **Fresh post-rebase end-to-end harness run against HEAD `0bc174ab03`** (refreshed 2026-05-20 after rebasing onto current `upstream/main` to absorb the `currentTurnKind` → `currentInboundEventKind` rename and the rest of the 1000+ adjacent commits since 2fcaab0010; the earlier `c76b3c19ec` proof is preserved in the gateway-runtime evidence above): `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` drives the actual production code paths (`enqueueSystemEvent` → `drainFormattedSystemEventBlock` → `stripInternalRuntimeContext`) on a throwaway sessionKey, with no mocks. Command: `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-pr72201.ts`. Live terminal output:
    ```
    === (A) post-enqueue queue snapshot ===
    [
      {
        "audience": "internal",
        "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
        "contextKey": "cron:proof-pr72201",
        "text": "Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only."
      }
    ]

    === (B) rendered prompt body (model-visible) ===
    <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    System: [2026-05-20T19:07:04Z] Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only.
    <<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>

    --- block.forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true

    --- wrap checks ---
      BEGIN delimiter present: true
      END delimiter present  : true
      END after BEGIN        : true
      canonical header line  : true
      event body present     : true
      forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true

    === (C) user-facing view (after stripInternalRuntimeContext) ===
    ""

    --- strip checks ---
      no BEGIN in user view  : true
      no event body leaked   : true

    === RESULT ===
    PASS
    ```
    All three claims verified end-to-end on the rebased HEAD: (A) the event is queued with `audience: "internal"` and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` (operator-side `peekSystemEventEntries` exposes it for debugging); (B) the prompt body the model sees is wrapped in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` … `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` with the canonical header lines and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` flowing back on the returned `FormattedSystemEventBlock`; (C) `stripInternalRuntimeContext` reduces the user-facing view to an empty string — nothing from the internal event leaks to the transcript / history reader / channel surface. The harness lives at `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` (local artifact, not pushed in this PR — output above is the captured terminal log).
- Observed result after fix: cron-awareness events queued after isolated cron delivery no longer reach user-facing chat surfaces. The isolated cron continues to deliver its own result on its configured channel (unchanged), and the main session sees the awareness event only as wrapped runtime context for agent reasoning — not as a fake `System (untrusted)` user bubble. Live runtime logs in `gateway.log` show no new error or stack trace lines from this code path on the patched daemon.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a side-by-side pre-patch vs post-patch screenshot of a Matrix client showing the absence of the `System (untrusted): cron run …` bubble — the negative-evidence form is hard to capture cleanly because the bubble simply stops appearing. The unit test in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` asserts the `audience: "internal"` is set at the producer site as the structural regression. The broader audience-routing primitive (additional producers beyond `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) was intentionally **not** migrated in this PR; that scope is deferred to follow-up case-by-case work per #69492.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- src/infra/system-events.test.ts` passes (34/34, +9 audience tests covering default, round-trip, equality, wrap shape, ordering, only-user-facing, only-internal, escape, end-to-end strip).
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` passes (47/47); existing assertion extended with `audience: "internal"`.
- [x] `pnpm build` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm check` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passes (per `feedback_openclaw_check_gaps.md`-style validation gap closure).
- [x] `codex review --base upstream/main` clean ("No discrete correctness issues were identified in the diff.").

## Pre-PR peer review

This proposal was peer-reviewed by `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -c reasoning_effort=high` before implementation. The peer review caught a contract-confusion issue between two earlier drafts (selective-drain vs wrap-on-drain) and pushed for the wrap-on-drain design landed here. Round-2 verdict: approve with PR-body wording tweak (avoid GitHub auto-close keywords for a partial fix; "Addresses part of #69492" is the framing applied here).

## Degree of testing

Lightly tested. Unit tests cover the primitive default, round-trip, equality, the wrap shape, ordering, edge cases, end-to-end strip via `stripInternalRuntimeContext`, and adversarial delimiter-token escaping. The single migrated producer has a regression assertion in the existing test. No e2e or live-channel coverage added.

target.title: feat: cursor-based pagination for chat.history + fix partial NO_REPLY…
target.body:
## Summary

- Implements cursor-based pagination for `chat.history` so external consumers
  (e.g. webchat) can load older messages page by page using a `before` index
  parameter. Closes #19060.
- Fixes partial `NO_REPLY` stream deltas (`"NO_"`, `"NO_REPL"`, etc.) being
  committed as real assistant messages when a stream resolves to a silent reply.
  Closes #13703.

## Changes

**Protocol / server**
- `chat.history` accepts optional `before: integer` — returns messages before
  that index
- Response now includes `cursor` (first index returned) and `hasMore`
- Cursor is recomputed after `capArrayByJsonBytes` / `enforceChatHistoryFinalBudget`
  to stay accurate when leading messages are byte-capped (`droppedFromFront`)
- Extracted `computeHistoryCursor` as a testable pure function

**UI controller**
- `ChatState` gains `chatCursor`, `chatHasMore`, `chatLoadingOlder`
- `loadOlderChatHistory`: fetches the previous page, prepends messages, 500 ms
  cooldown to avoid re-entry before scroll position restores
- `handleChatEvent` delta branch: skips NO_REPLY deltas (preserves existing
  stream); final/abort branches: also suppress partial NO_REPLY prefixes via
  `isPartialSilentReply`

**UI rendering**
- Removed `CHAT_HISTORY_RENDER_LIMIT = 200` which silently discarded all
  prepended older messages
- "Load older messages" button rendered at the top of the thread when
  `hasMore && onLoadOlder`
- Scroll position compensated after prepend via `compensateChatScrollForPrepend`

**Docs & tests**
- `docs/gateway/protocol.md`: documents `before`, `cursor`, `hasMore`,
  and byte-trimming behaviour

commands.author: SweetSophia

target.title: Strip leaked reasoning preambles before outbound send
target.body:
## Summary

This PR strips leaked plain-text reasoning preambles before outbound assistant replies are delivered to user-facing channels.

The current patch is intentionally narrow:

- routes delivery text through the canonical assistant-visible sanitizer from `sanitizeUserFacingText`
- strips high-confidence untagged reasoning preambles such as `I should...` and explicit `Let me think...` / `Let me reason...`
- preserves ordinary user-facing prose, including `Let me show...`, `The user...` summaries, fenced/schema examples, and unfenced CSS snippets
- does not reintroduce structural contamination, CSS, or footer cleanup into the delivery sanitizer

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Regression test

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agent runtime / reply delivery
- [x] Shared text sanitization
- [x] Tests

## Linked Issue/PR

Related to leaked reasoning preambles reaching Telegram and other outbound delivery surfaces.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The production delivery path uses `sanitizeUserFacingText`, while the earlier preamble cleaner was only present on `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`. That left plain-text reasoning preambles visible in real outbound messages even when sanitizer tests passed against the wrong boundary.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local verification:

```bash
CI=true corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts
```

Result on head `a5fe6aa1a328d28024498c9d57607445606b6655`:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       221 passed (221)
```

Additional sanity check:

```bash
git diff --check
```

Result: no whitespace errors.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

High-confidence leaked reasoning preambles are removed before delivery. Ordinary visible reply text that resembles documentation or instructional prose is preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

No new permissions, dependencies, workflow changes, network surfaces, or secret-handling paths are introduced.

The main risk is message-delivery compatibility: a sanitizer false positive could hide visible text, while a false negative could leave leaked preambles visible. The patch therefore keeps the heuristic narrow and adds preservation regressions for known false-positive shapes.

## Repro + Verification

The sanitizer is exercised through both:

- `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, the shared delivery profile
- `sanitizeUserFacingText`, the production-facing helper used before outbound delivery

Covered strip cases include:

- GLM-style planning lines paired with visible output
- `The user...` only when paired with explicit planning text
- `Let me think...` and `Let me reason...` reasoning preambles

Covered preservation cases include:

- ordinary `The user...` summaries
- ordinary `Let me show...` instructional prose
- first-person instructional prose
- unfenced CSS snippets
- fenced JSON schema examples

## Evidence

Local targeted tests pass on the current head.

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       221 passed (221)
```

Smoke output from the actual TypeScript sanitizer modules is included in the Real behavior proof section below.

## Human Verification (required)

I verified the focused sanitizer tests and direct smoke output locally. Live Telegram delivery from a deployed gateway on this exact PR head has not been captured yet; maintainers can request Mantis proof if transport-level confirmation is required.

## Review Conversations

Latest ClawSweeper feedback requested a narrow fix for explicit `let me think` / `let me reason` preambles without making generic `let me` prose strong evidence. This head implements that boundary and adds strip/preserve regressions.

## Compatibility / Migration

No migration required. The change affects outbound sanitizer behavior only.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **False positives:** Kept generic `let me`, `The user...`, CSS snippets, and schema examples preserved by tests.
- **False negatives:** Added targeted strong scoring for explicit reasoning phrases like `Let me think...` and `Let me reason...`.
- **Broad channel impact:** Kept the patch scoped to delivery-profile leaked-preamble cleanup only.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Leaked plain-text reasoning preambles are stripped through the user-facing delivery sanitizer, including explicit `Let me think...` / `Let me reason...` forms, while ordinary `Let me show...`, `The user...` summaries, and unfenced CSS snippets are preserved.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw workspace checkout on commit `a5fe6aa1a328d28024498c9d57607445606b6655`, using the actual TypeScript sanitizer modules through `corepack pnpm exec tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `corepack pnpm exec tsx` with direct imports from `sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` and `assistant-visible-text.ts`, invoking let-me-think, let-me-show, ordinary-user-summary, and unfenced-CSS cases.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the command above:

```json
{
  "letMeThink": "Visible answer.",
  "letMeShowKept": "Let me show you the setup.\n\nThen we can wire the deployment.",
  "userSummaryKept": "The user can configure the plugin from the settings screen.\nThe user should run the command again after saving.\n\nThis summary is user-facing and should remain intact.",
  "cssKept": "Use these styles:\ncolor: red;\nfont-family: sans-serif;\n\nThen reload the app."
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The explicit `Let me think...` reasoning preamble collapses to only `Visible answer.`; ordinary `Let me show...`, `The user...` summaries, and unfenced CSS snippets are unchanged.
- **Automated regression tests:** `CI=true corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` => 221 passed.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram delivery from a deployed gateway on this exact PR head; that still needs maintainer/Mantis proof or a live affected setup.

target.title: feat: implemented configurable label templates for spawned agent sess…
target.body:
Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                      
         
  - Problem: sessions_spawn accepted a plain label param with no convention or auto-prefix mechanism; in multi-agent pipelines (Manager / Extractor / Scorer / Reporter), the sessions list shows raw          
  user-supplied labels with no way to filter or identify which agent spawned a session.                                                                                                                        
  - Why it matters: Operators running multi-agent workflows must manually prefix every sessions_spawn call — fragile, discipline-dependent, and breaks when callers omit it.
  - What changed: Added a labelTemplate config field (string with {agentId} and {taskPreview} tokens) to both agents.defaults.subagents and per-agent agents.list[].subagents. When sessions_spawn is called   
  without an explicit label, the template fires as fallback. Per-agent config overrides defaults. Explicit label always wins.                                                                                  
  - What did NOT change: No change to spawn behavior, session lifecycle, registry, ACP path, or any existing config keys. Fully additive and backwards-compatible.
  
  Change Type                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  - Feature
  
   Linked Issue/PR
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  - Closes #72074                                           
  - This PR fixes a bug or regression

  Root Cause (if applicable)

  N/A — feature addition, not a bug.                                                                                                                                                                           
   
  - Root cause: N/A                                                                                                                                                                                            
  - Missing detection / guardrail: N/A                      
  - Contributing context: N/A                                                                                                                                                                                  
   
  Regression Test Plan (if applicable)                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                            
  N/A — no existing behavior changed.

  - Coverage level that should have caught this: N/A
  - Target test or file: N/A
  - If no new test is added, why not: Pure additive config + fallback path. No existing code path altered; template only fires when label is absent and labelTemplate is configured.                           
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  User-visible / Behavior Changes                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  New optional config field labelTemplate in agents.defaults.subagents and agents.list[].subagents. No change when field is absent (default behavior unchanged).                                               
   
  Example config:
  ```                                                                                                                                                                                              
  {                                                         
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "subagents": { "labelTemplate": "{agentId}-{taskPreview}" }                                                                                                                                            
      }                                                            
    }                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  }       
```                                                  
   
  Tokens: {agentId} = target agent id, {taskPreview} = first 40 chars of task (newlines stripped).
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Diagram
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Before:                                                   
  ```sessions_spawn(task, label?) -> label || ""  (no template)```
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  After:
 ``` 
 sessions_spawn(task, label?) -> explicitLabel                                                                                                                                                                
                               ?? perAgentTemplate(agentId, task)
                               ?? defaultsTemplate(agentId, task)                                                                                                                                              
                               ?? ""
  ```
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Security Impact                                           

  - New permissions/capabilities? No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
  - New/changed network calls? No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? No                                                                                                                                                                 
  - Data access scope changed? No
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Repro + Verification                                      

  Environment

  - OS: macOS 25.0.0                                                                                                                                                                                           
  - Runtime: Node 22+
  - Relevant config: agents.defaults.subagents.labelTemplate: "{agentId}-{taskPreview}"                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Steps
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  1. Set agents.defaults.subagents.labelTemplate to "{agentId}-{taskPreview}" in config.                                                                                                                       
  2. Invoke sessions_spawn with agentId: "scorer", task: "Score all candidates", no label.
  3. Check sessions list.                                                                                                                                                                                      
                                                            
  Expected                                                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                            
  - Session label: scorer-Score all candidates                                                                                                                                                                 
   
  Actual                                                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                            
  - Before fix: label is empty string.                                                                                                                                                                         
   
  Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                            
  - Failing test/log before + passing after
  - Trace/log snippets
  - Screenshot/recording
  - Perf numbers (if relevant)

  Code-reviewed manually; no runtime environment available for live verification (Node 18 on dev machine, repo requires Node 22+).                                                                             
   
  Human Verification                                                                                                                                                                                           
                                                            
  - Verified scenarios: config type additions, zod schema additions (both defaults + per-agent), template substitution logic, explicit label precedence, per-agent-over-defaults precedence.                   
  - Edge cases checked: no labelTemplate configured (returns ""), no agentId provided (token replaced with ""), task longer than 40 chars (truncated), task with newlines (stripped).
  - What I did not verify: live runtime spawn with actual session list inspection.                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Review Conversations                                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  - I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? Yes                                                                                                                                                                                   
  - Config/env changes? Yes — new optional fields added, no existing fields changed
  - Migration needed? No                                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                            
  Risks and Mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                            
  - Risk: Template with no tokens (e.g. "fixed-label") stamps every session with the same label.                                                                                                               
    - Mitigation: Acceptable — user-configured, documented behavior. Could add a lint warning later.
  - Risk: opts?.config is snapshotted at tool-construction time, not call time.                                                                                                                                
    - Mitigation: Consistent with all other opts?.config usage in the same function; not a regression.

target.title: [EV-002B] Skill Workshop parity: path, TOCTOU, prompt-budget hardening
target.body:
## Summary

Implements **EV-002B** (Skill Workshop defense-in-depth) per Evolves plan: gated procedural writer stays **only** in `extensions/skill-workshop`; no core `skills_manage` tool.

### Changes
- **G2 path:** `isPathInside` / `isPathInsideWithRealpath` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime` (re-export from `scan-paths`) for skill dir, SKILL.md, and support files.
- **G3 TOCTOU:** Second `prepareProposalWrite` immediately before `atomicWrite`; stale workspace throws `skill proposal stale: workspace changed before write`.
- **G4 economics:** `enforceSkillsPromptBudgetIfConfigured` uses `previewSkillsPromptImpact` / `buildSyntheticWorkspaceSkillEntryForPreview` (exported via `skills-runtime`) so queue and apply align with core skills prompt limits.
- **Docs:** `docs/plugins/skill-workshop.md` — security model, memory vs Workshop, maintenance grep note.
- **Tests:** pending no disk write, quarantine no SKILL.md, replace drift, POSIX symlink escape (`it.skipIf(win32)`).

### Not in this PR
- Research / learning-bridge telemetry on Workshop apply (deferred per EV-002B plan).

### Validation
- `pnpm test -- extensions/skill-workshop/index.test.ts` (23 tests)
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` + `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`

### Notes
- `resolveWorkspaceSkillPromptState` return type extended with `truncated`, `compact`, `skillsInPromptCount`, `skillsEligibleForPromptCount` so `previewSkillsPromptImpact` matches upstream `main` after merge.

target.title: Improve local gateway RPC diagnostics
target.body:
# Respect explicit null deviceIdentity and improve local gateway RPC error reporting

## Summary

This PR improves gateway RPC behavior in two ways:

1. It makes `GatewayClient` honor an explicit `deviceIdentity: null` instead of replacing it with an auto-loaded device identity.
2. It improves RPC failure reporting so early connection failures are not surfaced only as a generic `1006` close.

Fixes #65621.

## Motivation

Local RPC commands could fail with:

```text
gateway closed (1006 abnormal closure (no close frame)): no close reason
```

even when the real cause happened earlier in the connection flow.

This made it unnecessarily hard to distinguish:
- low-level socket/connect failures
- auth failures
- handshake timeouts
- device-auth / pairing-related behavior

Additionally, callers currently cannot reliably suppress device identity because explicit `null` is treated like an absent value.

## Changes

- preserve explicit `deviceIdentity: null` in `GatewayClient`
- avoid auto-loading device identity when the caller explicitly requested omission
- propagate early connect errors more clearly through the RPC failure path
- improve test coverage for local loopback RPC behavior

## Files of interest

- `dist/client-mAkhLNco.js`
- `dist/call-BzHcsP5K.js`

## Test coverage to add

- explicit `deviceIdentity: null` is preserved
- loopback RPC path with configured auth succeeds
- pre-handshake connect failures surface a meaningful error
- probe and normal RPC paths remain consistent and diagnosable

target.title: fix: redact secrets in /config show replies
target.body:
## Fix Summary
`/config show` exposed the raw parsed config object directly in chat replies, so owner-triggered config inspection could leak gateway tokens, provider API keys, and channel secrets into message history. This patch routes both full-config and path-specific `/config show` responses through the existing schema-aware config redaction pipeline before rendering the JSON reply.

## Issue Linkage
Fixes #65623

## Security Snapshot
- CVSS v3.1: 7.7 (High)
- CVSS v4.0: 8.3 (High)

## Implementation Details
### Files Changed
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-config.ts` (+6/-2)
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts` (+62/-0)

### Technical Analysis
The vulnerable `/config show` path in `handleConfigCommand` cloned `snapshot.parsed` and serialized it directly into the chat response. The fix now loads runtime schema UI hints and applies `redactConfigObject(...)` before either subtree lookup or full-object rendering, so the chat-command path matches the existing redaction guarantees already used by `config.get`.

## Validation Evidence
- Command: `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts`
- Status: passed

## Risk and Compatibility
- non-breaking; reply format is unchanged apart from replacing sensitive values with the standard redaction sentinel

## AI-Assisted Disclosure
- AI-assisted: yes
- Model: github-copilot/gpt-5.4

target.title: fix(agents): log pre-prompt compaction fits decisions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the pre-prompt auto-compaction precheck only logged recovery routes, so a `route=fits` decision looked the same as the precheck not running.
- Solution: format and emit one debug-level pre-prompt precheck line for every decision route, including `fits`.
- What changed: the log now includes route, estimated prompt tokens, prompt budget before reserve, overflow tokens, reducible tool-result chars, reserve/effective reserve, context budget, message counts, and session identity.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no compaction thresholds, pruning behavior, cache-ttl policy, or model routing changed.

## Motivation

#68609 is source-proven as an observability bug: the data already exists at the call site, but the normal `fits` path falls through silently. This is also the first useful diagnostic step for the broader #80594 runtime budget-awareness narrative, because it makes the pre-turn budget decision visible before changing policy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68609
- Related #80594
- Related #9409
- Related #54996
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: pre-prompt compaction precheck decisions now produce a debug log even when the route is `fits`, with the token fields requested in #68609.
- Real environment tested: local Windows source worktree on Node 24.14.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`.
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 2 files / 22 tests; oxfmt reported all 3 files correctly formatted; `git diff --check` passed; core production tsgo passed.
- Observed result after fix: `formatPrePromptPrecheckLog` covers `route=fits` and includes `estimatedPromptTokens`, `promptBudgetBeforeReserve`, `overflowTokens`, `toolResultReducibleChars`, `reserveTokens`, and `effectiveReserveTokens`; the pre-prompt call site invokes it for every non-skipped precheck result.
- What was not tested: a long-running live Anthropic Opus/cache-ttl session was not reproduced; this PR intentionally adds diagnostics before changing heuristic or pruning policy. Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was blocked because the selected local `crabbox` binary failed basic sanity checks and no sibling `../crabbox/bin/crabbox` was present.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the pre-prompt call site only logged the truncation and compaction branches, leaving `fits` decisions silent.
- Missing detection / guardrail: helper coverage already asserted `route=fits`, but no test pinned the human-readable diagnostic fields for a fits decision.
- Contributing context (if known): the mid-turn tool-result precheck already had an all-route debug log, but the pre-prompt path did not mirror that pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: formatting a `fits` pre-prompt precheck diagnostic includes route and budget fields.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the full embedded runner test is expensive and timed out locally; the extracted formatter is production code called by the runner.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing helper tests covered the `fits` result, but not the log fields.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Debug logs include an additional `[context-overflow-precheck] pre-prompt check ... route=fits` line when debug logging is enabled. Runtime behavior is unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
pre-prompt precheck -> route=fits -> prompt submission with no precheck log

After:
pre-prompt precheck -> route=fits -> debug budget log -> prompt submission
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows
- Runtime/container: Node 24.14.0, pnpm-installed source worktree
- Model/provider: mocked/unit path only
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Inspect pre-prompt call site in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`.
2. Verify `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` returns the requested fields for `route=fits`.
3. Run focused test, formatting, whitespace, and core production tsgo proof.

### Expected

- `route=fits` decisions are observable in debug logs with the budget fields from #68609.

### Actual

- The new formatter test pins the fits diagnostic line, and the call site logs it for every precheck result.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: focused fits diagnostic formatting, production call-site wiring, formatting, whitespace, core production typecheck.
- Edge cases checked: missing `sessionKey` falls back to `sessionId`/`unknown`; unwindowed message count only appears when supplied.
- What you did **not** verify: live long-session cache-ttl behavior and broad Testbox changed gate, due local Crabbox binary blocker noted above.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: extra debug line could be noisy in debug mode.
  - Mitigation: it is debug-level only and reuses the existing context-overflow precheck log namespace.

target.title: feat(plugins): add registerProviderRuntimeAuthOverride API
target.body:
## Summary

Add a public plugin registration API, `registerProviderRuntimeAuthOverride`, that lets external plugins provide runtime auth for specific built-in model providers without replacing the provider implementation.

This is intended for integrations that need brokered, short-lived, vault-backed, or proxy-routed credentials for built-in providers like `anthropic`, `openai`, or `openrouter`, and currently end up carrying downstream patches in `src/agents/model-auth.ts`.

## What Changed

- Added `api.registerProviderRuntimeAuthOverride({ providers, run })` to the plugin SDK.
- Added runtime resolution support in `src/agents/model-auth.ts`.
- Added a dedicated global module for active override registrations: `src/plugins/provider-runtime-auth-override-global.ts`.
  This keeps override state in sync with the active registry without widening the hook-runner global state.
- Added a reentrancy guard so a plugin override can safely call back into `resolveApiKeyForProvider()` via the public SDK helper without infinite recursion.
- Added plugin-id error logging when an override throws.
- Tightened `ProviderRuntimeAuthOverrideResult.mode` to the auth-mode union and kept runtime validation as defense-in-depth.
- Added an opt-in registration-based availability signal for callers that only need a coarse "may be available" answer, while keeping default availability checks conservative.

## API Shape

```ts
export type ProviderRuntimeAuthOverrideContext = {
  provider: string;
  modelId: string;
  profileId?: string;
};

export type ProviderRuntimeAuthOverrideResult = {
  apiKey: string;
  mode?: "api-key" | "oauth" | "token" | "aws-sdk";
  source?: string;
  baseUrl?: string;
  providerRequestHeaders?: Record<string, string>;
};

export type ProviderRuntimeAuthOverride = {
  providers: string[];
  run: (
    ctx: ProviderRuntimeAuthOverrideContext,
  ) => Promise<ProviderRuntimeAuthOverrideResult | null | undefined>;
};
```

Example:

```ts
api.registerProviderRuntimeAuthOverride({
  providers: ["anthropic", "openai", "openrouter"],
  run: async (ctx) => {
    const token = await resolveBrokeredToken(ctx.provider);
    return token
      ? {
          apiKey: token,
          source: "broker:runtime",
          providerRequestHeaders: { "x-session": sessionId() },
        }
      : null;
  },
});
```

## Semantics

1. OpenClaw resolves provider and model normally.
2. Registered overrides for that provider are evaluated in registration order.
3. The first non-null result wins.
4. `null` / `undefined` means "no override, continue default behavior."
5. Throw means fail the request after logging the plugin id; there is no implicit fallback.
6. Unknown provider ids in the registration list are ignored.
7. If an override calls `resolveApiKeyForProvider()` internally, the inner call skips overrides and falls through to normal resolution.

## Scope Notes

- `baseUrl` and `providerRequestHeaders` are applied in the main LLM inference runner path.
- Side paths such as media understanding, image generation, and TTS currently consume only the override `apiKey` in v1.
- `hasAvailableAuthForProvider()` stays conservative by default because overrides may legally return `null` at runtime; callers that only need a coarse UI signal can opt into treating registration as "may be available."
- `resolveModelAuthMode()` is intentionally unchanged in this PR. A meaningful override-aware mode result would need static mode metadata on the registration API rather than a heuristic.

## Why A Dedicated Global Module

This PR originally stored active override registrations alongside the hook-runner global state. During review and implementation, that proved too easy to conflate with unrelated hook-runner lifecycle concerns.

The final shape keeps the feature but moves override registration state into a dedicated singleton module:

- clearer ownership
- smaller surface area
- easier reasoning for tests and registry swaps
- no widening of `GlobalHookRunnerRegistry`

## Security Impact

- New capability: a trusted plugin can opt in to runtime auth overrides for listed providers.
- The override callback receives only `{ provider, modelId, profileId? }`.
- The API does not expose already-resolved secrets from other providers.
- Throwing is explicit failure, not silent fallback.
- Override activity and thrown plugin ids are logged.

## Verification

Focused regression coverage was added for:

- override applied
- provider mismatch
- null result fallthrough
- thrown override
- first non-null wins
- unknown mode normalization
- `baseUrl` and `providerRequestHeaders` passthrough
- reentrancy guard
- active-registry swap synchronization
- runtime reset synchronization
- `hasAvailableAuthForProvider()` with registered overrides
- dedicated global override singleton behavior

Local command run:

```bash
npx vitest run   src/agents/model-auth.test.ts   src/plugins/runtime.test.ts   src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts   src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts   src/plugins/provider-runtime-auth-override-global.test.ts
```

Result:

- 5 test files passed
- 76 tests passed

## Compatibility

- Fully additive. If no plugin registers an override, behavior is unchanged.
- No config migration required.

## Optional Follow-ups

1. Extend `baseUrl` / header override application consistently across side paths such as media understanding, image generation, and TTS.
2. Add plugin SDK docs with a concrete override example.
3. Consider explicit static metadata if `resolveModelAuthMode()` ever needs to reflect runtime overrides accurately.

target.title: fix(config): allow historyLimit: 0 in GroupChatSchema
target.body:
## Summary

`GroupChatSchema.historyLimit` uses `.positive()` which rejects `0`, while every other `historyLimit` schema in the codebase uses `.min(0)`. Setting `messages.groupChat.historyLimit: 0` (meaning "disable group chat history") causes a **silent config reload failure** — Zod rejects the value and the runtime falls back to the previously cached config with no prominent error.

Fixes #65305

## Root cause

In `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`:

```ts
// Line 367 (before fix) — GroupChatSchema: rejects 0
historyLimit: z.number().int().positive().optional(),

// Line 374 — DmConfigSchema: accepts 0
historyLimit: z.number().int().min(0).optional(),

// Line 433 — channel-level schema: accepts 0
historyLimit: z.number().int().min(0).optional(),
```

`GroupChatSchema` is the only outlier.

## Fix

`.positive()` → `.min(0)` on line 367. One token changed.

## Test plan

- Added `src/config/zod-schema.core.test.ts` with 3 cases:
  - [x] `GroupChatSchema` accepts `historyLimit: 0`
  - [x] `GroupChatSchema` accepts a positive `historyLimit`
  - [x] `GroupChatSchema` rejects a negative `historyLimit`
- All 3 tests pass (`pnpm test src/config/zod-schema.core.test.ts`)

target.title: feat(sessions): add effective session explain command
target.body:
Adds a focused `openclaw sessions explain <key>` command to inspect how a stored session resolves today.

### What changed
- adds `openclaw sessions explain <key>`
- supports both human-readable text output and `--json`
- resolves the session through the canonical session-store lookup path
- explains:
  - session key
  - agent id
  - backing store path
  - default provider/model baseline
  - persisted overrides
  - persisted runtime provider/model
  - persisted workspace binding (`spawnedWorkspaceDir`)
  - final effective provider/model
  - final effective workspace
  - simple resolution flags (`usesOverrides`, `usesRuntimeModelRef`, `usesPersistedWorkspace`)

### Why
OpenClaw already had the pieces to resolve a session's effective model/provider/workspace, but there was no focused CLI explain surface for a real stored session. This makes it easier to debug why a given session is behaving the way it is.

### Output modes
- text: readable troubleshooting summary
- json: machine-friendly payload for tooling

### Tests
- `src/commands/sessions.test.ts`

target.title: test: add regression tests for <final> tag stripping in UI message extraction
target.body:
## Summary

Adds regression tests to verify that the `extractText` and `extractTextCached` functions properly strip internal `<final>` and `</final>` assistant output tags from both `content` array and `text` property message formats in the Control UI.

## Context

Issue #65182 reports that `<final>` tags are leaking through to the Control UI chat surface as raw text. After thorough investigation:

- The tag-stripping pipeline (`stripReasoningTagsFromText` → `stripAssistantInternalScaffolding` → `stripThinkingTags` → `extractTextCached`) is working correctly for all tested message formats
- All existing tests pass
- The stripping correctly handles: single-line tags, multiline blocks, mixed `<think>`/`<final>` tags, closing-only tags, and tags in both `content` array and `text` property formats

## This PR

Adds 4 new test cases to `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts`:
- `strips <final> tags from assistant content` — basic content array format
- `strips multiline <final> blocks from assistant content` — multiline block format
- `strips mixed <think> and <final> tags from assistant content` — combined reasoning + final format  
- `strips <final> tags from assistant text property` — legacy text property format

These tests serve as regression prevention and document the expected stripping behavior through the full UI extraction pipeline.

## Testing

- All UI tests pass (`pnpm vitest run --config vitest.ui.config.ts`)
- All shared-core text tests pass
- No changes to production code

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#65182

target.title: fix(cli,sessions): make local model run stateless by default and keep transcript fallback profile-scoped
target.body:
## 요약

이 PR은 local one-shot model run correctness를 복구하기 위한 승격 패치셋이다.

포함된 변경은 두 가지다.

1. `infer model run --local`의 기본 동작을 stateless로 변경
   - 기본 one-shot은 ephemeral session을 사용
   - explicit `sessionId/sessionKey`가 있을 때만 reuse가 살아 있음

2. session store / transcript fallback root를 profile-scoped로 정렬
   - local one-shot 실행이 global `~/.openclaw/...`가 아니라
     profile/agent-dir 기준 persistence root를 사용

추가로, 위 두 패치가 `Orin-25`, `Orin-26` canary에서 노드 독립적으로 재현되었다는 기준선 문서를 함께 기록했다.

## 배경

기존 local one-shot model run은 암묵적으로 main session을 재사용할 수 있었고,
transcript fallback도 global root에 붙을 수 있었다.

그 결과:
- text / vision cross-turn contamination
- local canary와 unrelated global session의 결합
- one-shot probe semantics와 실제 persistence scope의 불일치

가 발생할 수 있었다.

이번 변경으로 기본 semantics를 다음처럼 고정한다.

- one-shot 기본: stateless
- 연속성: explicit opt-in
- persistence root: profile-scoped

## 포함 커밋

- `4a41951056` `fix(cli): make local model run stateless by default`
- `05b1862690` `fix(sessions): keep local transcript fallback profile-scoped`
- `b3392f307a` `docs(refactor): record local model run promotion baseline`

## 범위

포함:
- local model run one-shot session semantics
- transcript fallback persistence scope
- promotion baseline / patchset docs

제외:
- discovery/provider/plugin fast-path
- TTS/tool/silent-reply canary workarounds
- performance-only canary gates

## 검증

Focused tests:
- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` 관련 신규 assertion 통과
- `src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts` 관련 신규 assertion 통과

추가 참고:
- `transcript.test.ts` 전체 파일 런에서 기존 file mode (`0o600`) 가정 1건 실패가 남아 있으나,
  이번 패치와는 무관함

Canary validation:
- `Orin-25`, `Orin-26` 둘 다에서 재현 확인
- text only: 정상
- vision only: 정상
- mixed `vision -> text`: 정상
- explicit same-session reuse: 정상
- profile-local transcript/store 생성 확인

## 위험도

낮음.

이 PR은 local one-shot correctness와 persistence scope만 다룬다.
성능용 canary 우회나 provider/plugin shortcut은 포함하지 않는다.

target.title: Add final reply payloads plugin hook
target.body:
## Summary
- add a new `final_reply_payloads` plugin hook at the final reply assembly stage
- expose resolved `providerUsed`, `modelUsed`, `responseUsageMode`, and `responseUsageLine`
- let plugins modify finalized reply payloads before the usage footer is appended

## Why
Existing hooks can either see outbound text without stable resolved model context (`message_sending`) or intercept earlier dispatch paths that do not cover every final reply route (`reply_dispatch`).

This hook gives plugins a single reliable place to add things like a truthful `Model:` line next to `Usage:`.

target.title: fix(googlechat): accept add-on space lifecycle payload variants
target.body:
The Google Chat add-on payload parser only handled `chat.messagePayload`, rejecting valid `addedToSpacePayload` and `removedFromSpacePayload` events as "invalid payload".

Expand `parseGoogleChatInboundPayload` to recognise all three payload variants and map to correct event types (MESSAGE, ADDED_TO_SPACE, REMOVED_FROM_SPACE).

Partially addresses #65007

target.title: fix(amazon-bedrock): track literal contextWindow fallbacks with provenance flag
target.body:
## What

Adds `contextWindowIsLiteralDefault?: boolean` to `ModelDefinitionConfig`. Set to `true` only when Bedrock discovery falls back to the hardcoded `DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW` (`32000`) with no user-configured `defaultContextWindow`.

`resolveContextWindowInfo` uses this flag to mark `source` as `"default"` instead of `"model"`, enabling the guard warning to fire for Bedrock models that used the discovery-layer fallback.

## Why

Bedrock's AWS API does not expose token limits. Discovery pre-sets `contextWindow: 32000` → `source: "model"` → `shouldWarnDefault: false`. The provenance flag lets the runtime distinguish between:
- A real API-provided or user-configured value (`source: "model"`)
- A hardcoded fallback with no provenance (`source: "default"`)

## How

- **Type**: Added optional `contextWindowIsLiteralDefault` to `ModelDefinitionConfig`
- **Discovery**: Bedrock sets `true` only when using literal `DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW = 32000` and `config.defaultContextWindow` is not set
- **Runtime**: `resolveContextWindowInfo` checks the flag and uses `source: "default"` when `true`
- **Call sites**: Updated all 3 call sites to pass the new flag

## Tests

- context-window-guard: `source=default` when flag is true
- context-window-guard: `source=model` when flag is false
- bedrock discovery: `flag=false` when `defaultContextWindow` is configured
- bedrock discovery: `flag=true` when no `defaultContextWindow` is set
- bedrock inference profiles: inherit flag from foundation model

## Safety

- **Zero breaking changes**: flag is optional on shared type; other providers omit it
- **Provider-local**: only Bedrock sets this; other providers are unaffected
- **Backward-compatible**: existing behavior unchanged when flag is absent

Closes #64919

target.title: Policy: add tool metadata conformance and runtime checks
target.body:
# Policy: add tool metadata conformance and runtime checks

## Summary

This PR extends the bundled Policy plugin from channel conformance into governed tool metadata. It keeps policy as an authored conformance layer over existing OpenClaw surfaces: `policy.jsonc` states requirements, `TOOLS.md` is observed as evidence, doctor/policy checks report drift, and the optional runtime gate consumes the same evidence when explicitly enabled.

- Adds `tools.requireMetadata` policy support for `risk`, `sensitivity`, and `owner`.
- Observes `TOOLS.md` declarations as tool evidence, including source `oc://TOOLS.md/tools/<id>` references.
- Adds doctor/policy findings for missing or unknown tool metadata.
- Keeps `policy check` and `doctor --lint` read-only.
- Adds opt-in `plugins.entries.policy.config.runtimeToolPolicy` enforcement through the existing trusted tool policy hook.
- Persists structured plugin approval metadata through `plugin.approval.request` so policy hash, evidence hash, and target references survive the gateway approval boundary.

## Quick Start

Enable the bundled Policy plugin and author a policy requirement:

```bash
openclaw plugins enable policy
```

```jsonc
{
  "tools": {
    "requireMetadata": ["risk", "sensitivity", "owner"],
  },
}
```

A matching `TOOLS.md` declaration should carry the required metadata, for example:

```md
## deploy
risk: critical
sensitivity: restricted
owner: ops
```

Run policy-only checks during authoring:

```bash
openclaw policy check
openclaw policy check --json
```

The same findings are available through `openclaw doctor --lint` when the Policy plugin is enabled.

## Configure Policy

Policy config remains under `plugins.entries.policy.config`:

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "policy": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "enabled": true,
          "path": "policy.jsonc",
          "runtimeToolPolicy": false,
          "workspaceRepairs": false,
          "expectedHash": "sha256:...",
          "expectedAttestationHash": "sha256:...",
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

`runtimeToolPolicy` is config-owned rather than policy-owned so deleting or breaking `policy.jsonc` cannot silently disable the runtime gate after the operator has opted in.

## Accept Policy State

`policy check --json` emits the stable audit tuple:

```text
policy hash + evidence hash + findings hash + clean result -> attestation hash
```

The tool evidence is part of the workspace evidence hash. A clean result can be accepted by recording `attestation.policy.hash` as `expectedHash` and `attestation.attestationHash` as `expectedAttestationHash`.

When runtime tool policy requests approval, the approval metadata now preserves the same audit values separately from the human-readable approval message:

```json
{
  "policyHash": "sha256:...",
  "evidenceHash": "sha256:...",
  "attestationHash": "sha256:...",
  "target": "oc://TOOLS.md/tools/deploy"
}
```

## Findings

This PR adds these policy findings:

| Finding | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `policy/tools-missing-owner` | A governed tool declaration is missing owner metadata. |
| `policy/tools-missing-risk-level` | A governed tool declaration is missing risk metadata. |
| `policy/tools-unknown-risk-level` | A governed tool declaration uses an unknown risk value. |
| `policy/tools-missing-sensitivity-token` | A governed tool declaration is missing sensitivity metadata. |
| `policy/tools-unknown-sensitivity-token` | A governed tool declaration uses an unknown sensitivity value. |

Findings identify both sides of the decision: `target` points to the observed `TOOLS.md` declaration and `requirement` points to the authored policy requirement.

## Runtime Gate

`doctor --lint` and `policy check` remain read-only. `doctor --fix` does not edit `TOOLS.md` in this PR.

When `runtimeToolPolicy` is enabled, the Policy plugin registers a trusted tool policy. It:

- blocks tool calls if the enabled policy artifact is missing, invalid, or hash-mismatched;
- blocks governed tools whose required metadata is missing or invalid;
- asks for approval for governed tools marked `risk: critical` or `IRREVERSIBLE_EXTERNAL`;
- includes structured approval metadata with policy/evidence/attestation values and the target tool reference;
- otherwise lets the normal tool call path continue.

This PR does not add new approval UI, supervisor protocol, or gateway protocol concepts beyond preserving plugin approval metadata already produced by the runtime policy.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: With the Policy base now merged through #80407, this PR adds tool metadata conformance on top of the bundled Policy plugin, including `TOOLS.md` evidence, five tool finding IDs, opt-in `runtimeToolPolicy` enforcement, and structured approval metadata persistence through `plugin.approval.request`.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on current PR head `ae9cd92694fddbdcb2037fa833dbc8d78573edc3`, compared against current `origin/main` `a13468320c63573917c185db278f3d4e13389a78` after #80407 merged.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/runtime-tool-policy.test.ts --reporter=dot` (3 files / 45 tests, including unparseable `policy check --json` regression)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot` (3 files / 63 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/extension-import-boundaries.test.ts --reporter=dot` (1 file / 6 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/oc-path/src/oc-path/tests/parse.test.ts extensions/oc-path/src/oc-path/tests/scenarios/h2-block-split.test.ts --reporter=dot` (2 files / 34 tests)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-extensions-policy-oc-path-api.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader.test.ts -t 'writes bundled oc-path package aliases' --reporter=dot` (1 test, proves packaged dist `@openclaw/oc-path/api.js` alias creation)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-extensions-policy-oc-path-alias.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/runtime-tool-policy.test.ts test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts test/extension-import-boundaries.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts -t 'writes bundled oc-path package aliases' --reporter=dot"` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)
- deterministic before/after source probe recorded in `before.json`, `after.json`, and `summary.txt` on the proof artifact branch

Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed for policy CLI, including unparseable `policy check --json`, policy doctor registration, runtime tool policy, bundled health check loading, plugin approval gateway handling, extension import boundaries, oc-path Markdown H2 parsing, extension type contracts, and whitespace checks. The refreshed artifact shows `origin/main` has the merged Policy plugin/channel base but no tool metadata checks, no runtime tool policy, and no approval metadata schema, while PR head has all five registered tool finding IDs, docs rows for all five, runtime missing/unknown metadata blocks, approval-path metadata tests, gateway approval metadata persistence/broadcast coverage, and multiline `TOOLS.md` metadata parsing.

Observed result after fix: PR #80056 keeps legacy channel-only evidence hashes stable when no `TOOLS.md` evidence is scanned, registers policy tool checks, records tool evidence with `oc://TOOLS.md/tools/<id>` targets, parses both inline and documented multiline metadata under `### <tool>` headings, emits tool metadata findings with target/requirement references, requests approval for multiline `risk: critical` declarations in runtime policy mode, and preserves policy approval metadata across `plugin.approval.request`. Policy now imports `parseMd` from `@openclaw/oc-path/api.js` for Markdown H2 block parsing and line offsets; policy still owns the tool metadata grammar.

Current-head deterministic proof artifact:

![Fresh current-head proof for policy tool metadata, runtime gate, multiline metadata, and approval metadata](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-80056-fresh/pr-80056/pr-80056-policy-tool-metadata-runtime-before-after-proof.png)

Supporting artifact files:

- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-80056-fresh/pr-80056/summary.txt
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-80056-fresh/pr-80056/before.json
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-80056-fresh/pr-80056/after.json

What was not tested: no new approval UI, supervisor protocol, or gateway protocol UI surface; those remain intentionally out of scope for this PR. Full docs formatter currently reports an unrelated `docs/reference/templates/CLAUDE.md` formatting issue on this checkout; the policy docs table was formatted and the PR-specific docs finding is fixed.

## Related

- Channels-first policy conformance: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80407
- Model, network, and MCP conformance examples: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80783
- Runtime audit metadata and attestation enforcement: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/81104

<!-- policy-stack-links:start -->

## Policy stack links

This PR is part of the Policy 1.0 proof stack. Final category branches added after the runtime-audit layer:

- #81974 — Policy: add secrets auth provenance checks
- #81981 — Policy: add gateway exposure checks

Full stack reference:

1. #80407 — Policy: add conformance system with channel checks
2. #80056 — Policy: add tool metadata conformance and runtime checks
3. #80783 — Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
4. #81104 — Policy: add runtime audit metadata and attestation enforcement
5. #81974 — Policy: add secrets auth provenance checks
6. #81981 — Policy: add gateway exposure checks

<!-- policy-stack-links:end -->

target.title: fix(codex): bridge computer use elicitations
target.body:
## Summary

- Computer Use MCP app-approval elicitations now route through the existing plugin approval bridge instead of falling through to the default decline path.
- Manual chat `/approve plugin:<id> <decision>` now resolves through the trusted operator approval runtime, so active plugin approvals are not hidden as `unknown or expired approval id` when they were created by a different gateway connection.
- Slash-command docs and the changelog now describe plugin approval support for `/approve`.

## Motivation

Computer Use app approvals need to reach the existing approval UI, and Slack manual approval commands need to resolve those pending plugin approvals from the same trusted runtime path native approval buttons already use.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs

## Scope

- [x] Codex app-server / Computer Use elicitation bridge
- [x] Gateway approval runtime
- [x] Slack/manual slash-command approval path
- [x] Docs / changelog

## Root Cause

Computer Use app approvals were not represented in `pluginAppPolicyContext`, so they missed the native plugin policy path and fell through to default MCP elicitation decline. After bridge approval delivery worked, manual `/approve plugin:<id>` still used raw transient `callGateway`; that client had `operator.approvals` scope but was not marked as `internal.approvalRuntime`, so gateway approval visibility filtered out active plugin approval records created by another requester connection.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Computer Use app approvals can reach the plugin approval bridge, and authorized manual `/approve plugin:<id>` commands resolve through the trusted approval runtime instead of seeing active plugin approvals as unknown or expired.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on branch `codex/computer-use-elicitation-bridge`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts src/infra/approval-gateway-resolver.test.ts`; `python3 /Users/kevinlin/.codex/skills/specy/scripts/validate_flow_doc.py --kind flow-doc --doc .mem/main/specs/16-slack-manual-plugin-approve-runtime/flows/manual-text-plugin-approval.md`; `pnpm docs:list`; `git diff --check`; Mermaid CLI render of `.mem/main/specs/16-slack-manual-plugin-approve-runtime/flows/manual-text-plugin-approval.md`.

Evidence after fix: Focused approval Vitest output reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 23 passed (23)` locally and in the verification subagent. Flow-doc validation reported `PASS [flow-doc] .../manual-text-plugin-approval.md`. `git diff --check` exited 0 with no output. `pnpm docs:list` completed successfully.

Observed result after fix: Unit coverage verifies manual `/approve` calls `resolveApprovalOverGateway`, plugin approval routes pass `resolveMethod: "plugin"`, exec-first legacy fallback still falls through to plugin on not-found, unauthorized senders do not call the resolver, and gateway-scope failures remain blocked.

What was not tested: The live dev-profile flow that opens Apple Notes through Computer Use was not rerun after this manual approval runtime commit; previous focused bridge proof remains unit/build based.

## Regression Test Plan

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts` and `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` cover the Computer Use elicitation bridge shape.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts` now covers the manual `/approve` resolver call shape and fallback behavior.
- `src/infra/approval-gateway-resolver.test.ts` covers the trusted gateway resolver behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Computer Use app approval prompts can be accepted through the existing approval bridge. Manual `/approve plugin:<id>` commands can resolve active plugin approval prompts from Slack and other shared text-command channels when the sender is authorized.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No

The bridge still requires an approval decision before Computer Use proceeds. Manual text approval does not weaken gateway approval visibility policy; it uses the same trusted operator approval runtime credential path as native approval UIs.
